messages.content: write "星の光に―思いをかけて―熱い銀河を―胸に抱けば―夢はいつしか―この手に届く―" in english

Casting thoughts upon the starlight,
Embracing the fiery Milky Way in our hearts,
Before long, our dreams will come within our reach.

write "星の光に　思いをかけて
熱い銀河を　胸に抱けば
夢はいつしか　この手に届く" in english

Upon the light of stars, thoughts are cast,
Embrace the fervent Milky Way in your heart,
And soon enough, dreams will come to grasp.
is it accurate

The translation I provided captures the essence and poetic nature of the original Japanese text, but translating poetry often requires a balance between literal accuracy and maintaining the poetic feel. Here's a slightly more direct translation that stays close to the original text:

Casting thoughts to the light of the stars,
If the fiery galaxy is embraced in the chest,
Dreams will, before long, reach these hands.

The translation tries to retain the original line structure and metaphorical content, which is typical of creative expressions like poetry.
what does "思いをかけて" mean

The phrase "思いをかけて" in Japanese translates literally to "casting thoughts" or "placing one's thoughts/emotions" on something. In this context, it might connote the idea of sending one's wishes or hopes toward the light of the stars, similar to making a wish upon a star. It implies a heartfelt yearning or deep contemplation directed at something that inspires awe or hope, like the stars in the night sky.
can you use some other word than casting thoughts

Sure! Instead of "casting thoughts," you could use the phrase "sending wishes" to convey a similar sentiment. If we replace that in the translation, it would read:

"Sending wishes to the starlight,
If you embrace the fiery Milky Way in your heart,
Dreams will eventually come within reach of your hands."

This version maintains the emotional and aspirational tone of the original text while offering an alternative to "casting thoughts."
what about a different word

Of course! Another option could be "projecting hopes," which carries a similar meaning. Here’s how it would look in the translation:

"Projecting hopes upon the light of the stars,
When you hold the fiery galaxy close to your heart,
Dreams will, before long, be within your grasp."

This retains the essence of sending one's desires towards something greater, in this case, the stars, and emphasizes the heartfelt intention behind the phrase.
what about a different word

Certainly! If you're looking for another word to replace "casting thoughts," let's try "focusing dreams":

"Focusing dreams on the light of the stars,
Cradling the fiery Milky Way in one's heart,
Dreams will, before long, be within reach of these hands."

This alternative emphasizes the idea of concentrating one's aspirations or dreams toward the stars.
what about start with "to the light of the stars" and try to go from there

"To the light of the stars, where wishes take flight,
Embracing the fiery Milky Way in one's heart,
Dreams, in time, will be grasped by these hands."
use something else instead of "where wishes take flight"

"To the light of the stars, my yearnings I entrust,
With the blazing galaxy held close to my chest,
Dreams will in time be captured by these hands."
can you use something that would be musical at the end of the first phrase, "my yearnings i entrust

"To the light of the stars, my yearnings ascend,
As the fiery galaxy tight in my embrace,
Dreams, in their own time, to these hands will extend."
start with "to the light of the stars, our hopes are"

"To the light of the stars, our hopes are bestowed,
Cradling the warm Milky Way at our hearts' behest,
Dreams will, in due course, to our hands unfold."
use another word instead of bestowed to make it musical

"To the light of the stars, our hopes are unfurled,
Embracing the warm Milky Way against the breast,
In time, our dreams into our hands will be curled."
use another word instead of "unfurled" to remain to the original translation

"To the light of the stars, our hopes are aligned,
Embracing the warm Milky Way close to our hearts,
In time, dreams in our hands will be intertwined."
